claims.text: On 17 May 2026 the WHO Director-General determined that the epidemic of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the DRC and Uganda constitutes a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC).
As of 17 June 2026, the Democratic Republic of the Congo had reported 896 confirmed cases of Bundibugyo virus disease including 232 deaths.

As of 29 June 2026, the Democratic Republic of Congo reported 1,333 confirmed cases and 399 deaths (up 26 cases and 22 deaths from the previous report); Uganda reported 20 confirmed cases and 2 deaths as of 30 June 2026, with 15 recoveries and no new cases since 21 June.

description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo declared its 17th Ebola outbreak — caused by Bundibugyo virus — on 15 May 2026, centred in Ituri Province. On 17 May 2026 the WHO Director-General declared the DRC and Uganda epidemic a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC). Confirmed cases have continued to climb through late June.

impact.summary: As of 29 June 2026 the DRC had reported 1,333 confirmed cases including 399 deaths; Uganda reported 20 confirmed cases and two deaths as of 30 June 2026, with no new Ugandan cases since 21 June.

location.region: Ituri Province (Mongbwalu), DRC, with cases in Uganda

name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) — DR Congo & Uganda
